title: Preparing E. coli cryo cultures
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"r7lj","text":"This protocol describes how to prepare bacteria for further storage at -80 °C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"2j7al","text":"Always work under sterile conditions to avoid contamination.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"c0ig1","text":"Always wear appropriate protection equipment while working with liquid nitrogen or dry ice.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Aliquot ~99% glycerol and autoclave. You can also used filtered sterile glycerol. Glycerol Contributed by users Catalog #G5516

Incubate your desired Escherichia coli cultures overnight until the culture reaches a exponential to stationary phase and a minimum absorbance of 1.0 and maximum absorbance of 5.0 at OD 600 . 16:00:00, Incubation (overnight)
Inoculate 3 mL of your desired media containing appropriate antibiotics with your desired Escherichia coli strain. Work under sterile conditions to avoid contamination. undetermined, Media (i.e. LB)
Centrifuge your well grown cultures for 10 minutes at 4,000 rpm. 00:10:00, Centrifugation at 4,000 rpm
Discard the supernatant quickly. Do not disturb the pellet.
Resuspend your pellet in 800 µl of your desired media with specific antibiotics. undetermined, media (i.e. LB)

Pipette 200 µl sterile glycerol in a cryo culture tube. Add 800 µl of your desired resuspended pellet/culture (20 % glycerol (v/v)). You can also use glycerol concentrations up to 40 % (v/v). Most labs store bacteria in 15-25 % glycerol. undetermined, glycerol undetermined, bacteria culture
(Alternative: Freeze your cryo cultures with dry ice or liquid nitrogen.) Store your cryo culture at -80 °C.
creator: Miriam D
